summary: Verified P2 fixes from iteration_2. 4 of 5 fixes PASS: FIX 2 (login error), FIX 3 (signup error), FIX 4 (shared release constants), FIX 5 (CLI install command). FIX 1 (GetDesktopHeroCard mobile layout) is INCOMPLETE — flex-col md:flex-row was correctly added to the inner card, but the root cause of the letter-by-letter wrap is that the dashboard sidebar (~240px) does NOT collapse on mobile/tablet viewports, leaving main content only ~103px at 375px and the hero card title still wraps badly. All 3 P0 regressions (admin login, popovers, sidebar nav x8) still pass.

ui_bugs.issue: Sidebar (aside, 240px wide) is visible and non-collapsible at 375px AND 768px viewports. Main content is squeezed to ~103px at 375px and ~500px at 768px. This causes GetDesktopHeroCard title 'GET AETHER FOR WINDOWS' to still wrap letter-by-letter at 375px and word-by-word at 768px — even though the card's own flex-col md:flex-row fix was applied correctly. The fix needs to be at the Layout level (collapse/hide sidebar below md or lg breakpoint, OR provide a hamburger drawer).

issues: Sidebar consumes 240px of 375px viewport, leaving ~135px for main content — entire dashboard is unusable on mobile (hero title wraps letter-by-letter, demo mode banner same, all cards stack into narrow column).
Sidebar still visible (240px). Hero title still wraps to 4+ lines because md:flex-row activates exactly at 768px and right-side button group takes ~270px, leaving only ~150px for title.

action_items: P1 — Fix dashboard layout to collapse/hide the sidebar below md (or lg) breakpoint. Add a mobile hamburger toggle. This is the ACTUAL root cause of the hero-card letter-wrap (the card's own responsive classes are correct, but its container is too narrow).

Alternative quick fix: change GetDesktopHeroCard breakpoint from `md:flex-row` to `lg:flex-row` and `md:w-auto` to `lg:w-auto` so the card stays stacked until 1024px. Still leaves the broader sidebar-on-mobile issue.
FIX 2 (login error), FIX 3 (signup error), FIX 4 (shared releases.js), FIX 5 (CLI installCmd) all verified working — no further action.

Optional regression script improvement: Desktop popover platform-build data-testids could not be auto-discovered by my selector pattern; recommend the desktop popover use the same data-testid pattern as CLI (e.g. desktop-download-{platform}).
critical_code_review_comments: lib/releases.js correctly centralizes version/URL constants; DownloadDesktopButton, DownloadCliButton, GetDesktopHeroCard all import from it. ✓
DownloadCliButton installCmd now correctly uses GitHub Release URL via PowerShell iwr (no more get.aether.sh ghost host). ✓
Login & Signup error elements now have data-testid + role='alert' + class='error' (visible, accessible). ✓

However GetDesktopHeroCard's flex-col md:flex-row is necessary but NOT SUFFICIENT — the dashboard parent layout still presents the sidebar at all viewports. The card cannot lay itself out properly when its container is 103px wide. This is a Layout.jsx / Sidebar.jsx concern, not a card concern.
All 8 sidebar routes render content (>200 chars). No console errors introduced by responsive changes (only the pre-existing /api/auth/me 401 noise noted in iteration_2).

success_rate.frontend: FIX 1 broken root-cause, FIX 2-5 PASS = 4/5 = 80%; regressions 100%

context_for_next_testing_agent: FIX 1 root cause clarified: it's the sidebar, not the hero card. Hero card's flex-col md:flex-row is in place at line 58 of GetDesktopHeroCard.jsx. The real fix lives in Layout.jsx / Sidebar.jsx — collapse sidebar below lg (1024px) and add a drawer toggle. After main agent applies the layout fix, re-test at 375/768/1024/1920 verifying: (a) sidebar hides/drawer at <lg, (b) hero card width matches viewport at 375, (c) title 'GET AETHER FOR WINDOWS' fits on 1-2 lines at 375px. Other 4 fixes are confirmed shipping-ready.
rca_of_the_issue: GetDesktopHeroCard mobile bug — Root Cause Update: The card's own flex-col md:flex-row fix is correctly applied. BUT the dashboard layout always renders a 240px fixed sidebar at every viewport (no md: breakpoint to hide it). At 375px viewport: 375 - 240 (sidebar) = ~135px for main content area, with px-4 padding the hero card becomes 103px wide. At this width even a perfectly responsive flex-col card cannot avoid letter-by-letter title wrap. Reproduction: login as admin → resize to 375px → observe sidebar still occupies left third. Mitigation: in Layout.jsx wrap `<aside>` with `hidden lg:flex` (or `hidden md:flex`) and add a mobile drawer triggered by a hamburger button in the header.
